title: Retention Check: The Protocol Feature Mental Model

questions.q: State the protocol feature mental model in one sentence.
questions.a: Every protocol encodes a trust assumption required for it to function, and that same trusted input is the mechanism an attacker exploits.

questions.q: What is the specific ARP design feature that enables ARP spoofing?
questions.a: ARP trusts that the last reply to a broadcast is authoritative, with no authentication. Any host on the LAN can send an unsolicited reply claiming ownership of any IP.

questions.q: What TCP feature enables RST injection, and what is the outcome of a successful attack?
questions.a: TCP accepts RST segments without cryptographic verification; an attacker who guesses the approximate sequence number can terminate any established TCP connection.

questions.q: Name two bash commands you would run to verify ARP cache integrity on a Linux host during an investigation.
questions.a: `arp -n` (show current ARP cache) and `ip neigh show` (netlink equivalent). Compare entries to DHCP logs or known MAC-to-IP mappings to identify duplicates or unexpected MAC addresses.
